entity.name: Harsh Ankur
entity.description: I am a full-stack open source maintainer focused on building high-performance document processing infrastructure. Since 2019, I have been the sole architect of officeParser, maintaining it through six major versions and ensuring its stability for a global user base that depends on it for over 10 million downloads and 260,000+ weekly installations.

projects.name: officeParser
projects.description: officeParser is a strictly-typed, zero-dependency-core library for Node.js and the browser that transforms complex office documents (DOCX, PDF, XLSX, etc.) into a unified, hierarchical Abstract Syntax Tree (AST). It is a critical piece of infrastructure for AI ingestion pipelines, search engines, and document-management tools, providing the only JavaScript/TypeScript solution that maintains format parity across both server and client runtimes.

channels.description: GitHub Sponsors - Recursive monthly support for long-term project stability.
PayPal - Direct contributions for immediate infrastructure needs.
Buy Me A Coffee - Micro-contributions from the developer community.

plans.name: Core Sustainability & Maintenance
plans.description: Focuses on the baseline health of the library: maintaining compatibility with Node.js LTS releases, resolving security vulnerabilities, and providing timely triage for issues across the 8 supported file formats. This ensures reliability for the 260k+ weekly users currently reaching it.

plans.name: The Plugin Ecosystem (AI Foundations)
plans.description: Funds the development of a composable plugin architecture (v7 design). This allows officeParser to remain lightweight while enabling high-value transformations like Markdown/HTML serialisation and LLM-ready chunking. This work is critical for cementing the library as the default choice for modern AI/RAG ingestion in JavaScript.

plans.name: Intelligent Document Ingestion
plans.description: The 'Full Focus' program. Enables advanced vertical features such as semantic table reconstruction (headers/merged-cells) and structured chart-to-JSON extraction. Also includes officially validated drivers for Bun, Deno, and Edge environments, and first-party connectors for LangChain and LlamaIndex.

history.description: All development was contributed voluntarily alongside full-time employment.
